values: my family's heirloom
a rare tonic
something I've been saving
a blade that's seen better days but still cuts true
the last of my silver
a map to something valuable
an old favor I can call in
a set of tools worth more than coin
passage on the next caravan
a recipe known only to my family
access to our private stores
a word with someone important
a charm that's brought me luck
the finest leather I've ever worked
something the previous owner left behind
a contact who can open doors for you
a piece of armor that's saved my life twice
knowledge that most would kill for
a sealed crate from the old kingdom
supplies that'll keep you going through the worst of it
a ring my mother wore
an old hunting knife with a bone handle
a jar of salve that heals overnight
the best cut of dried venison you'll ever taste
a spool of wire strong enough for snares
a cloak that sheds rain like oilskin
a pouch of herbs worth their weight in gold
a compass that's never steered me wrong
a bottle of something the soldiers pay double for
an iron key to a chest I've never opened
a bundle of arrows fletched by the best bowyer I know
a lantern that burns twice as long as any other
the deed to a plot of land nobody else wants
a signed letter of introduction to the guild master
a bag of nails: not glamorous, but worth plenty right now
a wedge of cheese aged longer than most marriages
a blanket woven tight enough to stop wind
a fishing line that won't snap
a pair of boots that still have life in them
a hand-drawn map of the caves up north
a candle that burns blue when danger's near
an old coin the collectors would fight over
a flask of oil the smiths swear by
a whetstone from the quarry they closed
a belt buckle with a hidden compartment
a tinderbox that lights in the rain
a waterskin lined with something that keeps the taste fresh
the real story of what happened at the crossing
a name that'll get you through the next checkpoint
a place to sleep where nobody asks questions
the best meal you've had in months
a warm bath and clean clothes
a roof over your head for the winter
my spot on the next trading run
first pick of the harvest
a seat at the council meeting
a stall at the market, rent-free for a season
the right to fish the deep pool
a share of whatever the next hunt brings in
a barrel of mead I've been aging for a wedding that never happened
a bolt of cloth dyed the color they can't get anymore
a stone from the old shrine: some say it's lucky
a feather from something I couldn't identify
a tooth on a cord that wards off nightmares, or so I'm told
the glass eye of a dead fortune-teller
a small wooden box that hums when you hold it
an ink that changes color with the temperature
a bone needle that never dulls
a mirror that shows things slightly different than they are
a knot of rope that can't be untied
a thimble made of some metal I've never seen
a rag that cleans anything
a whistle carved from antler
the right to use my name when you need a reference
a story that'll make sense when you need it to
something I can't explain but you'll understand when you see it
whatever I pull out of the river tomorrow
the truth about why the last person in your position left
a warning worth more than payment
directions to a place most people don't know exists
safe passage through territory that isn't mine to promise, but I'll make it work
a cure for what's going around
a salted pork leg that'll last you a fortnight
a bundle of dried firewood, already split
enough flour for a week of bread
a jar of rendered fat for cooking
a coil of good rope, not the cheap stuff
a pot of pine tar for waterproofing
a sack of turnips, ugly but filling
a clay jug of honey from my own hives
a stack of clean bandages and a sewing needle
a small cask of vinegar for preserving
a basket of dried mushrooms
a pound of tallow candles
three days' worth of hardtack
a bag of oats and a feeding nose-bag
a skin of goat's milk, fresh this morning
a bundle of torches, pitch-dipped and ready
a handful of iron nails and a hammer
a ball of twine long enough to mark a trail
a dozen fish hooks and a length of gut
a small keg of lamp oil
two woolen blankets, only slightly moth-eaten
a bag of charcoal for the forge
a jar of pickling brine and the spices to go with it
a leather satchel with a working buckle
a pair of heavy gloves for handling thorns
a belt pouch with a drawstring that actually closes
a clay pipe and a pinch of something to smoke
a comb of beeswax and a scraping knife
a wooden bowl that won't crack in the cold
a horse blanket in better shape than it looks
a sharpened hatchet head, just needs a handle
a coil of copper wire for mending pots
a piece of flint the size of your fist
a strip of rawhide for binding
a cake of soap made with lavender
a wooden spoon carved from cherry
a stoppered bottle of spirits for cleaning wounds
a small grinding stone, barely used
a pouch of salt, which is harder to come by than you'd think
a stack of rough-cut shingles for patching a roof
a length of chain with every link intact
an awl and a bobbin of waxed thread
a tin cup that nests inside a tin pot
a folding knife with a locking blade
a piece of smoked eel wrapped in leaves
a horn comb and a scrap of mirror
a belt of cured leather, pre-punched
a working mouse trap, already baited
a slab of pemmican that'll outlast us both
a basket of duck eggs, still warm
a jar of bone glue and a brush
a foot-bellows for starting stubborn fires
enough seed to plant a kitchen row
a padded vest that stops the worst of the wind
a promise from the blacksmith to sharpen your tools for free
an introduction to the herbalist who won't see strangers
a word to the ferryman to let you cross without charge
a letter to my cousin in the next town: she'll put you up
the location of a cache I buried when things got bad
a debt marker from someone who actually pays
my endorsement to the merchant's guild
a recommendation to the captain of the guard
a standing invitation to the harvest feast
permission to forage in the elder's private woodlot
a discount at the only shop in town that matters
the combination to the strongbox in the old office
a token that'll get you past the bridge toll
free stabling for your animals whenever you pass through
use of the workshop when the smith isn't there
borrowing rights at the tool library
a voucher for the bathhouse, good for a month
a favor from the miller: your grain ground first, every time
a marked map showing where the deer bed down in winter
first refusal on the next litter of pups
a claim stake for the new diggings, if they pan out
a hand-copied page from the healer's recipe book
a sample of the ore the prospector found last week
a jar of the dye nobody else can get the recipe for
a bag of the seeds from the apple tree that survived the frost
a cutting from the vine that produces twice the yield
a pot of the grease the wheelwright uses: lasts three times longer
a square of the canvas the sailmaker calls indestructible
a bottle of the stuff the farrier puts on cracked hooves
a pinch of the powder the rat-catcher swears by
a measure of the clay the potter keeps locked up
a length of the gut string the bowyer says outperforms sinew
a lump of the wax the chandler won't sell to just anyone
a thimbleful of the spice the cook paid a fortune for
a chip of the stone the mason says rings like a bell
a strand of the fiber the weaver calls stronger than hemp
a daub of the pitch the roofer mixes himself
a dose of the tincture the midwife keeps for emergencies
a spoonful of the starter the brewer guards with his life
a scrap of the leather the saddler says is from the old country
a few drops of the ink the scribe makes from oak galls
a nugget of the solder the tinker uses for clean joins
a clipping from the hedge that flowers in winter
a pressed flower from a plant I've never seen bloom twice
a handful of gravel from the streamed where the gold was
a broken arrowhead that's older than this settlement
a brass button from a uniform nobody recognizes
a scrap of parchment with writing I can't read
a wooden token with a mark that opens one particular door
an old military ration tin, still sealed
a horseshoe the farrier swears is lucky
a jar of rendered beeswax, pure and golden
a set of bone dice older than anyone in town
a copper brooch shaped like a leaf
a drawstring bag of dried berries that keep indefinitely
a scrap of sailcloth you could patch a tent with
a carved walking stick with a hidden blade
a tin of boot grease that keeps water out for a month
a flat stone that skips seven times every throw
a hand-forged fish hook the size of your thumb
a lock of hair tied with silver thread: don't ask
a bag of goose down, cleaner than anything you'll find at market
a cork float and a sinker, ready for the line
